tu.prop: Because of the long timescale involved, however, the underlying dynamic processes responsible for the evolution of dune fields remain poorly understood8.

tuv.seg: Field studies of barchans--crescent-shaped dunes that propagate over solid ground under conditions of unidirectional wind1 --have long focused on the investigation of an equilibrium between sand transport by wind and the control of air flow by dune topography2­4, which are thought to control dune morphology and kinematics5­7.

Les études de terrain réalisées sur les barkhanes, des dunes en forme de croissant de lune qui se propagent sur terrain ferme en situation de vent unidirectionnel1, se sont longtemps focalisées sur la recherche d'un équilibre entre le transport du sable par le vent, d'une part, et le contrôle des flux éoliens régis par la topographie24 des dunes, d'autre part. Ces deux phénomènes étant considérés comme majeurs dans le contrôle de la morphologie et de la cinématique57 des dunes.

tu.prop: Field studies of barchans--crescent-shaped dunes that propagate over solid ground under conditions of unidirectional wind1 --have long focused on the investigation of an equilibrium between sand transport by wind and the control of air flow by dune topography2­4, which are thought to control dune morphology and kinematics5­7.

Here we combine data from a three-year field study in the Moroccan Sahara with a model study to show that barchans are fundamentally unstable and do not necessarily behave like stable solitary waves, as suggested previously9­12.

tuv.seg: Because of the long timescale involved, however, the underlying dynamic processes responsible for the evolution of dune fields remain poorly understood8.
Cependant, en raison des longues échelles de temps impliquées, les processus dynamiques sous-jacents responsables de l'évolution des champs de dunes demeurent mal compris.

tu.prop: Because of the long timescale involved, however, the underlying dynamic processes responsible for the evolution of dune fields remain poorly understood8.
We find that dune collisions and changes in wind direction destabilize the dunes and generate surface waves on the barchans.

tuv.seg: Here we combine data from a three-year field study in the Moroccan Sahara with a model study to show that barchans are fundamentally unstable and do not necessarily behave like stable solitary waves, as suggested previously9­12.

Cet article s'appuiera, d'une part, sur des données relevées lors d'une étude de terrain effectuée dans le Sahara marocain sur une durée de trois ans, et d'autre part, sur les données tirées d'une modélisation [effectuée en laboratoire] afin de démontrer que les barkhanes sont des structures fondamentalement instables et qu’elles ne se comportent pas nécessairement de la même manière que des ondes solitaires stables, contrairement à ce que les études précédentes avaient suggéré912.

tu.prop: Here we combine data from a three-year field study in the Moroccan Sahara with a model study to show that barchans are fundamentally unstable and do not necessarily behave like stable solitary waves, as suggested previously9­12.
Because the resulting surface waves propagate at a higher speed than the dunes themselves, they can produce a series of new barchans of elementary size by breaking the horns of large dunes.

tuv.seg: We find that dune collisions and changes in wind direction destabilize the dunes and generate surface waves on the barchans.
Nous avons constaté que les collisions entre dunes ainsi que les changements de direction du vent déstabilisent les barkhanes et génèrent des ondes de surface sur les barkhanes.

tu.prop: We find that dune collisions and changes in wind direction destabilize the dunes and generate surface waves on the barchans.

The creation of these new dunes provides a mechanism for sand loss that prevents dune fields from merging into a single giant dune and therefore plays a fundamental role in the control of size selection and the development of dune patterns.

tuv.seg: Because the resulting surface waves propagate at a higher speed than the dunes themselves, they can produce a series of new barchans of elementary size by breaking the horns of large dunes.

Ces ondes de surface se propageant à une vitesse plus élevées que les dunes qui leur servent d'hôtes, elles produisent elles-mêmes une série de nouvelles barkhanes de taille élémentaire, en engendrant une scission des grandes dunes.

tu.prop: Because the resulting surface waves propagate at a higher speed than the dunes themselves, they can produce a series of new barchans of elementary size by breaking the horns of large dunes.
Very few barchans in a dune field exhibit the smooth crescent shapes that are simulated in models (Fig. 1e); instead they display more complex substructures.

tuv.seg: The creation of these new dunes provides a mechanism for sand loss that prevents dune fields from merging into a single giant dune and therefore plays a fundamental role in the control of size selection and the development of dune patterns.

La création de ces nouvelles dunes fournit un mécanisme de perte de sable qui empêche les champs de dunes de se fondre en une seule dune géante et joue donc un rôle fondamental dans le contrôle de la sélection des tailles et le développement des motifs des dunes.

tu.prop: The creation of these new dunes provides a mechanism for sand loss that prevents dune fields from merging into a single giant dune and therefore plays a fundamental role in the control of size selection and the development of dune patterns.
The windward slope and flanks of barchans generally present superimposed bed-forms which can become high enough to induce air-flow separation and thus secondary avalanche slip faces (Fig. 1b­d).

tuv.seg: Very few barchans in a dune field exhibit the smooth crescent shapes that are simulated in models (Fig. 1e); instead they display more complex substructures.

Seul un nombre très limité de barkhanes au sein d'un champ de dunes présente la forme stéréotypée traditionnellement modélisée en laboratoire (croissant de lune avec une surface lisse, cf. Fig.1e). En effet, les barkhanes présentent souvent des sous-structures bien plus complexes.

tu.prop: Very few barchans in a dune field exhibit the smooth crescent shapes that are simulated in models (Fig. 1e); instead they display more complex substructures.

On the basis of the numerical findings that two colliding barchans can cross through one another while still preserving their shape, these phenomena have been interpreted as small dunes climbing onto large ones9­11.

tuv.seg: The windward slope and flanks of barchans generally present superimposed bed-forms which can become high enough to induce air-flow separation and thus secondary avalanche slip faces (Fig. 1b­d).

La pente exposée au vent, ainsi que les flancs des barkhanes, présentent généralement des formes de lits de sable superposés, susceptibles d'atteindre une telle hauteur qu'ils peuvent induire une séparation des flux d'air, exposant des faces entières de la dune à l'effondrement par avalanche (cf. Fig. 1bd).

tu.prop: The windward slope and flanks of barchans generally present superimposed bed-forms which can become high enough to induce air-flow separation and thus secondary avalanche slip faces (Fig. 1b­d).
This is contradicted by our direct field investigation, during which we have followed the birth, growth, propagation and further evolution of these structures.

tuv.seg: On the basis of the numerical findings that two colliding barchans can cross through one another while still preserving their shape, these phenomena have been interpreted as small dunes climbing onto large ones9­11.

Sur la base des résultats numériques selon lesquels deux barkhanes entrant en collision peuvent se croiser tout en conservant leur forme, ces phénomènes ont été interprétés comme de petites dunes grimpant sur des dunes plus grandes911.

tu.prop: On the basis of the numerical findings that two colliding barchans can cross through one another while still preserving their shape, these phenomena have been interpreted as small dunes climbing onto large ones9­11.

We studied more than a hundred barchans in the region between Tarfaya (278 56 0 N, 2128 56 0 W), Sidi Aghfinir (288 06 0 N, 2128 03 0 W) and Laayoune (278 10 0 N, 2138 14 0 W), where the wind regime (wind rose on Fig. 1) is one of the most unimodal13.

tuv.seg: This is contradicted by our direct field investigation, during which we have followed the birth, growth, propagation and further evolution of these structures.
Ces interprétations sont contredites par notre étude de terrain, au cours de laquelle nous avons suivi la naissance, la croissance, la propagation et l'évolution de ces structures.

tu.prop: This is contradicted by our direct field investigation, during which we have followed the birth, growth, propagation and further evolution of these structures.
Two situations under which the dune surface becomes destabilized are identified: changes of wind direction (Fig. 1a­d) and collisions (Fig. 1f­i).

tuv.seg: We studied more than a hundred barchans in the region between Tarfaya (278 56 0 N, 2128 56 0 W), Sidi Aghfinir (288 06 0 N, 2128 03 0 W) and Laayoune (278 10 0 N, 2138 14 0 W), where the wind regime (wind rose on Fig. 1) is one of the most unimodal13.

Nous avons étudié plus d'une centaine de barkhanes dans la région située entre Tarfaya (278 56 0 N, 2128 56 0 W), Sidi Aghfinir (288 06 0 N, 2128 03 0 W) et Laayoune (278 10 0 N, 2138 14 0 W), où le régime éolien (rose des vents, cf. Fig. 1) est l'un des plus unimodaux13.

tu.prop: We studied more than a hundred barchans in the region between Tarfaya (278 56 0 N, 2128 56 0 W), Sidi Aghfinir (288 06 0 N, 2128 03 0 W) and Laayoune (278 10 0 N, 2138 14 0 W), where the wind regime (wind rose on Fig. 1) is one of the most unimodal13.
We have precisely investigated the detailed nature of the unstable modes on five dunes displaying well-defined patterns.

tuv.seg: Two situations under which the dune surface becomes destabilized are identified: changes of wind direction (Fig. 1a­d) and collisions (Fig. 1f­i).
Nous avons identifié deux types de situations entraînant la déstabilisation de la surface des dunes : les changements de direction du vent (cf. Fig. 1ad), et les collisions entre dunes (cf. Fig.fi).

tu.prop: Two situations under which the dune surface becomes destabilized are identified: changes of wind direction (Fig. 1a­d) and collisions (Fig. 1f­i).

With the help of fixed markers, we observed that the undulations propagate downwind on the stoss (windward) slope and the flanks of the dune at a velocity c < 2 m day21, which is typically ten times larger than that of the dune itself.

tuv.seg: We have precisely investigated the detailed nature of the unstable modes on five dunes displaying well-defined patterns.
Nous avons plus précisément examiné la nature complexe des modes instables sur cinq dunes portant des motifs bien définis.

tu.prop: We have precisely investigated the detailed nature of the unstable modes on five dunes displaying well-defined patterns.
Their wavelength and amplitude do not vary much in the course of their motion.

tuv.seg: With the help of fixed markers, we observed that the undulations propagate downwind on the stoss (windward) slope and the flanks of the dune at a velocity c < 2 m day21, which is typically ten times larger than that of the dune itself.

A l'aide de marqueurs fixes, nous avons pu observer que les ondulations se propagent dans le sens du vent sur la pente exposée au vent à une vitesse de c < 2 m par jour21. Cette vitesse est typiquement dix fois plus grande que celle de la dune elle-même.

tu.prop: With the help of fixed markers, we observed that the undulations propagate downwind on the stoss (windward) slope and the flanks of the dune at a velocity c < 2 m day21, which is typically ten times larger than that of the dune itself.
We have measured the variations of height dh and sand flux dq on a cut line along a barchan horn (Fig. 2b).

tuv.seg: Their wavelength and amplitude do not vary much in the course of their motion.
Ni leur longueur d'onde ni leur amplitude ne varie de manière remarquable au cours de leur déplacement.

tu.prop: Their wavelength and amplitude do not vary much in the course of their motion.
These quantities are proportional, which demonstrates that these undulations behave as plane propagating waves.

tuv.seg: We have measured the variations of height dh and sand flux dq on a cut line along a barchan horn (Fig. 2b).
Nous avons mesuré les variations de la hauteur dh et du flux de sable dq sur une ligne de coupe le long d'une corne de barkhane (cf. Fig. 2b).

tu.prop: We have measured the variations of height dh and sand flux dq on a cut line along a barchan horn (Fig. 2b).

tuv.seg: These quantities are proportional, which demonstrates that these undulations behave as plane propagating waves.
Ces quantités sont proportionnelles, ce qui démontre que ces ondulations se propagent de la même manière que des ondes planes.

tu.prop: These quantities are proportional, which demonstrates that these undulations behave as plane propagating waves.

The nucleation and propagation of such waves on a sand bed is governed by the interaction between the bed profile, which modifies the air flow, and the sand transport, which controls the erosion and deposition processes.
Along the upwind side of a hump, the streamlines converge, yielding an increasing wind and thus an increasing flux, so that erosion takes place2­4.

tuv.seg: The nucleation and propagation of such waves on a sand bed is governed by the interaction between the bed profile, which modifies the air flow, and the sand transport, which controls the erosion and deposition processes.

La nucléation et la propagation de telles ondes sur un lit de sable sont régies par l’interaction entre le profil du lit, qui modifie le flux d'air, et le transport du sable, qui contrôle les processus d'érosion et de dépôt.

tu.prop: The nucleation and propagation of such waves on a sand bed is governed by the interaction between the bed profile, which modifies the air flow, and the sand transport, which controls the erosion and deposition processes.
Conversely, the flux decreases on the downwind side causing accretion, which in total means that the bump translates downwind.

tuv.seg: Along the upwind side of a hump, the streamlines converge, yielding an increasing wind and thus an increasing flux, so that erosion takes place2­4.
Le long du côté d'une bosse au vent, les lignes de courant convergent, entraînant une augmentation du vent et donc un flux croissant, ce qui provoque une érosion24.

tu.prop: Along the upwind side of a hump, the streamlines converge, yielding an increasing wind and thus an increasing flux, so that erosion takes place2­4.
However, the accretion does not start precisely at the crest but is shifted upwind, so that the bump is amplified.

tuv.seg: Conversely, the flux decreases on the downwind side causing accretion, which in total means that the bump translates downwind.
À l'inverse, le flux diminue du côté sous le vent, provoquant une accrétion, ce qui signifie en fin de compte que la bosse se déplace dans le sens du vent.

tu.prop: Conversely, the flux decreases on the downwind side causing accretion, which in total means that the bump translates downwind.
This instability mechanism is directly related to the asymmetry of the wind flow, which originates in the nonlinear inertial term of the Navier­Stokes equations.

tuv.seg: However, the accretion does not start precisely at the crest but is shifted upwind, so that the bump is amplified.
Néanmoins, l'accumulation ne débute pas exactement à la crête de la dune, mais est déplacée sous le vent, grossissant alors la bosse.

tu.prop: However, the accretion does not start precisely at the crest but is shifted upwind, so that the bump is amplified.

So far in this description, no length scale is involved, because the atmospheric boundary layer is fully turbulent, and the mechanism therefore predicts an unconditional instability at all wavelengths.

tuv.seg: This instability mechanism is directly related to the asymmetry of the wind flow, which originates in the nonlinear inertial term of the Navier­Stokes equations.
Ce mécanisme d’instabilité est directement relié à l’asymétrie de l’écoulement du vent, qui trouve son origine dans le terme inertiel non linéaire des équations de Navier-Stokes.

tu.prop: This instability mechanism is directly related to the asymmetry of the wind flow, which originates in the nonlinear inertial term of the Navier­Stokes equations.
There is however a smallscale cut-off for the instability related to the transient approach to saturation of the sand flux.

tuv.seg: So far in this description, no length scale is involved, because the atmospheric boundary layer is fully turbulent, and the mechanism therefore predicts an unconditional instability at all wavelengths.

Jusqu'à présent dans cette description, aucune échelle de longueur n’a encore été prise en compte, car la couche limite atmosphérique est entièrement turbulente, et le mécanisme prédit donc une instabilité inconditionnelle pour toutes les longueurs d’ondes.

tu.prop: So far in this description, no length scale is involved, because the atmospheric boundary layer is fully turbulent, and the mechanism therefore predicts an unconditional instability at all wavelengths.
As exemplified in Fig. 3b, the flux reaches its equilibrium value, determined by the wind strength, over a characteristic distance L called the saturation length.

tuv.seg: There is however a smallscale cut-off for the instability related to the transient approach to saturation of the sand flux.
La coupure à petite échelle de cette instabilité est cependant liée à l'approche transitoire de la saturation du flux de sable.

tu.prop: There is however a smallscale cut-off for the instability related to the transient approach to saturation of the sand flux.
This effect shifts downwind the position at which the flux q is maximum, and thus stabilizes small bumps.

tuv.seg: As exemplified in Fig. 3b, the flux reaches its equilibrium value, determined by the wind strength, over a characteristic distance L called the saturation length.
Comme démontré dans le graphique 3b, le flux atteint son point d'équilibre, déterminé par la force du vent, quand il dépasse la distance caractéristique L appelée longueur de saturation.

tu.prop: As exemplified in Fig. 3b, the flux reaches its equilibrium value, determined by the wind strength, over a characteristic distance L called the saturation length.
Previously8, we have derived a simple model (called8 CC ) accountC ing for all the above mechanisms: mass conservation, shape-flux coupling and flux saturation.

tuv.seg: This effect shifts downwind the position at which the flux q is maximum, and thus stabilizes small bumps.
Avec cet effet, la position à laquelle le flux q est à son maximum est décalée dans le sens du vent, stabilisant ainsi les petites bosses.

tu.prop: This effect shifts downwind the position at which the flux q is maximum, and thus stabilizes small bumps.
Here we present field measurements of the quantities characterizing the unstable waves--wavelength, propagation speed and amplitude--and quantitatively compare them to the predictions of the model.

tuv.seg: Previously8, we have derived a simple model (called8 CC ) accountC ing for all the above mechanisms: mass conservation, shape-flux coupling and flux saturation.
Précédemment8, nous avons établi un modèle simple (appelé8 CC) pour tous les mécanismes décrits ci-dessus : la conservation de masse, le couplage forme-flux et la saturation de l'écoulement.

tu.prop: Previously8, we have derived a simple model (called8 CC ) accountC ing for all the above mechanisms: mass conservation, shape-flux coupling and flux saturation.
Three dimensionless parameters A, B and D enter the model, which respectively govern the wind acceleration on a bump, the displacement upwind of the maximum velocity and the lateral coupling.

tuv.seg: Here we present field measurements of the quantities characterizing the unstable waves--wavelength, propagation speed and amplitude--and quantitatively compare them to the predictions of the model.

Nous présentons ici les mesures prisent sur le terrain des différentes grandeurs qui caractérisent les ondes instables (la longueur d’onde, la vitesse de propagation et l’amplitude) et leur comparaison quantitative avec les prévisions du modèle.

tu.prop: Here we present field measurements of the quantities characterizing the unstable waves--wavelength, propagation speed and amplitude--and quantitatively compare them to the predictions of the model.
Their values are tuned to reproduce the relations between the morphological parameters of barchans: height, width and length (Fig. 1e).

tuv.seg: Three dimensionless parameters A, B and D enter the model, which respectively govern the wind acceleration on a bump, the displacement upwind of the maximum velocity and the lateral coupling.

Trois paramètres sans dimension A, B et D interviennent dans le calcul du modèle, influençant respectivement l’accélération du vent sur une bosse, le déplacement en amont de la vitesse maximale et le couplage latéral.

tu.prop: Three dimensionless parameters A, B and D enter the model, which respectively govern the wind acceleration on a bump, the displacement upwind of the maximum velocity and the lateral coupling.
The only timescale of the problem is related to the saturated flux Q on a flat bed and encodes the wind strength (wind roses on Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: Their values are tuned to reproduce the relations between the morphological parameters of barchans: height, width and length (Fig. 1e).
Leurs valeurs sont ajustées pour reproduire les rapports entre les différents paramètres morphologiques des barkhanes : la hauteur, la largeur et la longueur (cf. Fig. 1e).

tu.prop: Their values are tuned to reproduce the relations between the morphological parameters of barchans: height, width and length (Fig. 1e).

The only characteristic length scale14 is the saturation length L introduced above, and we designed a specific experiment to measure it on a 20-m-long and 3-m-wide flat sand sheet prepared with a bulldozer (Fig. 3b).

tuv.seg: The only timescale of the problem is related to the saturated flux Q on a flat bed and encodes the wind strength (wind roses on Fig. 1).
La seule échelle de temps du problème est liée au flux saturé Q sur le lit plat et reflète l'intensité du vent (roses des vents, cf. Fig. 1).

tu.prop: The only timescale of the problem is related to the saturated flux Q on a flat bed and encodes the wind strength (wind roses on Fig. 1).

L is directly related to the length needed for a grain to reach the wind velocity, which scales15 with the grain density to fluid density ratio times the grain diameter d. Using the measured values L ¼ 1.7 m and d ¼ 180 mm, we obtain: rsand d ð1Þ L<4 rair in close agreement with our re-analysis of Bagnold's experiment1 (L ¼ 2.3 m for d ¼ 250 mm).

tuv.seg: The only characteristic length scale14 is the saturation length L introduced above, and we designed a specific experiment to measure it on a 20-m-long and 3-m-wide flat sand sheet prepared with a bulldozer (Fig. 3b).

La seule échelle de longueur caractéristique14 est la longueur de saturation L mentionnée ci-dessus, et nous avons créé un test spécifique pour la mesurer sur un lit de sable aplani à l’aide d’un bulldozer (cf. Fig. 3b), de 20m de longueur et de 3m de largeur (cf. Fig. 3b).

tu.prop: The only characteristic length scale14 is the saturation length L introduced above, and we designed a specific experiment to measure it on a 20-m-long and 3-m-wide flat sand sheet prepared with a bulldozer (Fig. 3b).
To derive the dispersion relation of surface waves, we have examined large dunes for which the scale separation is sufficient for us to consider that they are flat at the scale of the waves.

tuv.seg: L is directly related to the length needed for a grain to reach the wind velocity, which scales15 with the grain density to fluid density ratio times the grain diameter d. Using the measured values L ¼ 1.7 m and d ¼ 180 mm, we obtain: rsand d ð1Þ L<4 rair in close agreement with our re-analysis of Bagnold's experiment1 (L ¼ 2.3 m for d ¼ 250 mm).

La longueur L est directement liée à la longueur nécessaire pour qu'un grain atteigne la vitesse du vent, qui est proportionnelle15 au rapport entre la densité du grain et celle du fluide multiplié par le diamètre du grain d. En utilisant les valeurs mesurées L ¼ 1,7 m et d ¼ 180 mm, nous obtenons : rsand d ð1Þ L < 4 rair, en accord étroit avec notre nouvelle analyse de l'expérience de Bagnold1 (L ¼ 2,3 m pour d ¼ 250 mm).

tu.prop: L is directly related to the length needed for a grain to reach the wind velocity, which scales15 with the grain density to fluid density ratio times the grain diameter d. Using the measured values L ¼ 1.7 m and d ¼ 180 mm, we obtain: rsand d ð1Þ L<4 rair in close agreement with our re-analysis of Bagnold's experiment1 (L ¼ 2.3 m for d ¼ 250 mm).
Figure 1 | Response of barchan dunes to changes of wind direction and to collisions.

tuv.seg: To derive the dispersion relation of surface waves, we have examined large dunes for which the scale separation is sufficient for us to consider that they are flat at the scale of the waves.

Afin d'établir la relation de dispersion des ondes de surface, nous avons examiné de grandes dunes pour lesquelles la séparation des échelles est suffisante pour que l’on considère qu’elles sont plates à l’échelle des ondes.

tu.prop: To derive the dispersion relation of surface waves, we have examined large dunes for which the scale separation is sufficient for us to consider that they are flat at the scale of the waves.
Coloured contours extracted from GPS data are superimposed to aerial photographs taken from a plane or a kite (Supplementary Information).

tuv.seg: Figure 1 | Response of barchan dunes to changes of wind direction and to collisions.
Figure 1 | Réaction des barkhanes aux changements de direction du vent et aux collisions.

tu.prop: Figure 1 | Response of barchan dunes to changes of wind direction and to collisions.
The averaged sand flux rose (1999, Q ¼ 80 m2 yr21) shows a narrow unimodal regime whose dominant direction, indicated by yellow arrows, is the average direction of barchan motion.

tuv.seg: Coloured contours extracted from GPS data are superimposed to aerial photographs taken from a plane or a kite (Supplementary Information).
Les contours colorés extraits des données GPS sont superposés à des photographies aériennes prises à partir d'un avion ou d'un cerf-volant (cf. Informations complémentaires).

tu.prop: Coloured contours extracted from GPS data are superimposed to aerial photographs taken from a plane or a kite (Supplementary Information).

The maximum sand flux rose reveals a more isotropic distribution of extreme events (Supplementary Information). a­d, Response of barchan dunes to changes of wind direction. a, Two profiles of a small dune (purple, t ¼ 215 months; green, t ¼ 212 months) superimposed on an aerial photograph (t ¼ 0).

tuv.seg: The averaged sand flux rose (1999, Q ¼ 80 m2 yr21) shows a narrow unimodal regime whose dominant direction, indicated by yellow arrows, is the average direction of barchan motion.

La rose des flux de sable moyennés (1999, Q ¼ 80 m2 an21) montre un régime unimodal étroit dont la direction dominante, indiquée par des flèches jaunes, est la direction moyenne du déplacement des barkhanes.

tu.prop: The averaged sand flux rose (1999, Q ¼ 80 m2 yr21) shows a narrow unimodal regime whose dominant direction, indicated by yellow arrows, is the average direction of barchan motion.

The slip face quickly readapts to changes of wind direction (corresponding arrows). b, Surface waves on a medium-sized dune, induced by a northwest wind (reference profile at t ¼ 280 months). c, Destabilization of a medium-sized dune leading to the emission of elementary barchans (t ¼ 50 months).

tuv.seg: The maximum sand flux rose reveals a more isotropic distribution of extreme events (Supplementary Information). a­d, Response of barchan dunes to changes of wind direction. a, Two profiles of a small dune (purple, t ¼ 215 months; green, t ¼ 212 months) superimposed on an aerial photograph (t ¼ 0).

La rose des flux de sable maximale révèle une distribution plus isotrope des événements extrêmes (cf. Informations Supplémentaires). a-d, Réaction des barkhanes aux changements de direction du vent. a, Deux profils d'une petite dune (violet, t ¼ 215 mois ; vert, t ¼ 212 mois) superposés sur une photographie aérienne (t ¼ 0).

tu.prop: The maximum sand flux rose reveals a more isotropic distribution of extreme events (Supplementary Information). a­d, Response of barchan dunes to changes of wind direction. a, Two profiles of a small dune (purple, t ¼ 215 months; green, t ¼ 212 months) superimposed on an aerial photograph (t ¼ 0).

tuv.seg: The slip face quickly readapts to changes of wind direction (corresponding arrows). b, Surface waves on a medium-sized dune, induced by a northwest wind (reference profile at t ¼ 280 months). c, Destabilization of a medium-sized dune leading to the emission of elementary barchans (t ¼ 50 months).

La face de glissement se réadapte rapidement aux changements de direction du vent (flèches correspondantes). b, Ondes de surface sur une dune de taille moyenne, induites par un vent nord-ouest (profil de référence à t ¼ 280 mois). c, Déstabilisation d'une dune de taille moyenne conduisant à l'émission de barkhanes élémentaires (t ¼ 50 mois).

tu.prop: The slip face quickly readapts to changes of wind direction (corresponding arrows). b, Surface waves on a medium-sized dune, induced by a northwest wind (reference profile at t ¼ 280 months). c, Destabilization of a medium-sized dune leading to the emission of elementary barchans (t ¼ 50 months).
The surface waves are permanently driven by daily wind variations. e, Numerical dune produced by the CC C model8.

tuv.seg: d, Mega-barchan (t ¼ 350 months).
d, Méga-barkhane (t ¼ 350 mois).

tu.prop: The parameters (A ¼ 8.4, B ¼ 8.0, D ¼ 0.4) are tuned to get the same height, length and width as on c. f­h, Response of barchan dunes to collisions. f, Edging collisions between small dunes (yellow, t ¼ 17 months, green, t ¼ 23 months, purple, t ¼ 33 months).

tuv.seg: The surface waves are permanently driven by daily wind variations. e, Numerical dune produced by the CC C model8.
Les ondes de surface sont entraînées en permanence par les variations quotidiennes du vent. e, Dune numérique produite par le modèle CC C8.

tu.prop: The surface waves are permanently driven by daily wind variations. e, Numerical dune produced by the CC C model8.

Note also a fusion (red arrow). g, Coaxial collision of medium-sized dunes leading to a larger dune with a wake of new elementary barchans (t ¼ 60 months). h, Chain destabilization of medium-sized dunes (t ¼ 60 months).

tuv.seg: The parameters (A ¼ 8.4, B ¼ 8.0, D ¼ 0.4) are tuned to get the same height, length and width as on c. f­h, Response of barchan dunes to collisions. f, Edging collisions between small dunes (yellow, t ¼ 17 months, green, t ¼ 23 months, purple, t ¼ 33 months).

Les paramètres (A ¼ 8,4, B ¼ 8,0, D ¼ 0,4) sont ajustés pour obtenir la même hauteur, longueur et largeur que sur c. f­h, Réaction des barkhanes aux collisions. f, Collisions par les bords entre petites dunes (jaune, t ¼ 17 mois ; vert, t ¼ 23 mois ; violet, t ¼ 33 mois).

tu.prop: The parameters (A ¼ 8.4, B ¼ 8.0, D ¼ 0.4) are tuned to get the same height, length and width as on c. f­h, Response of barchan dunes to collisions. f, Edging collisions between small dunes (yellow, t ¼ 17 months, green, t ¼ 23 months, purple, t ¼ 33 months).
The trail of a dune sustains the instability of the next one. i, Long-term (t ¼ 350 months) evolution of a barchan field.

tuv.seg: Note also a fusion (red arrow). g, Coaxial collision of medium-sized dunes leading to a larger dune with a wake of new elementary barchans (t ¼ 60 months). h, Chain destabilization of medium-sized dunes (t ¼ 60 months).

On note également une fusion (flèche rouge). g, Collision coaxiale de dunes de taille moyenne conduisant à une dune plus grande avec un sillage de nouvelles barkhanes élémentaires (t ¼ 60 mois). h, Déstabilisation en chaîne de dunes de taille moyenne (t ¼ 60 mois).

tu.prop: Note also a fusion (red arrow). g, Coaxial collision of medium-sized dunes leading to a larger dune with a wake of new elementary barchans (t ¼ 60 months). h, Chain destabilization of medium-sized dunes (t ¼ 60 months).
Even though the largest dunes (highlighted dashed contours) persist, many dunes appear or disappear, showing that dune destabilization is an essential process of large-scale rearrangement.

tuv.seg: The trail of a dune sustains the instability of the next one. i, Long-term (t ¼ 350 months) evolution of a barchan field.
Le sillage d'une dune entretient l'instabilité de la suivante. i, Evolution à long terme (t ¼ 350 mois) d'un champ de barkhanes.

tu.prop: The trail of a dune sustains the instability of the next one. i, Long-term (t ¼ 350 months) evolution of a barchan field.

In the framework of the CC model, the linear stability analysis of a uniform C sand bed predicts an unconditional instability at large wavelengths of growth rate j and propagation speed c given by: B 2 AjkjL Qjkj 2D c ¼ ðA þ BjkjLÞ ð2Þ j ¼ Qk2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 where k ¼ 2p=l is the mode wavenumber.

tuv.seg: Even though the largest dunes (highlighted dashed contours) persist, many dunes appear or disappear, showing that dune destabilization is an essential process of large-scale rearrangement.

Même si les plus grandes dunes (contours en pointillés soulignés) persistent, de nombreuses dunes apparaissent ou disparaissent, montrant que la déstabilisation des dunes est un processus essentiel de réarrangement à grande échelle.

tu.prop: Even though the largest dunes (highlighted dashed contours) persist, many dunes appear or disappear, showing that dune destabilization is an essential process of large-scale rearrangement.

As all modes propagate downwind, the instability is of convective type16 so that the pattern develops spatially: any disturbance such as a change of wind direction or a colliding dune (Fig. 1) constitutes a seed amplified by the linear instability in the course of propagation.

tuv.seg: In the framework of the CC model, the linear stability analysis of a uniform C sand bed predicts an unconditional instability at large wavelengths of growth rate j and propagation speed c given by: B 2 AjkjL Qjkj 2D c ¼ ðA þ BjkjLÞ ð2Þ j ¼ Qk2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 where k ¼ 2p=l is the mode wavenumber.

Dans le cadre du modèle CC, l'analyse de stabilité linéaire d'un lit de sable uniforme en C prédit une instabilité inconditionnelle aux grandes longueurs d'onde du taux de croissance j et de la vitesse de propagation c données par : B 2 AjkjL Qjkj 2D c ¼ ðA þ BjkjLÞ ð2Þ j ¼ Qk2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 où k ¼ 2p = l est le nombre d'ondes de mode.

tu.prop: In the framework of the CC model, the linear stability analysis of a uniform C sand bed predicts an unconditional instability at large wavelengths of growth rate j and propagation speed c given by: B 2 AjkjL Qjkj 2D c ¼ ðA þ BjkjLÞ ð2Þ j ¼ Qk2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 1 þ ðkLÞ2 where k ¼ 2p=l is the mode wavenumber.

The maximum growth rate--the most unstable mode predicted by equation (2)--is reached for a wavelength l max < 12L < 20 m. l was measured systematically in a zone of 500 regular barchans (1­12 m high).

tuv.seg: As all modes propagate downwind, the instability is of convective type16 so that the pattern develops spatially: any disturbance such as a change of wind direction or a colliding dune (Fig. 1) constitutes a seed amplified by the linear instability in the course of propagation.

Comme tous les modes se propagent sous le vent, l'instabilité est de type convectif16 de sorte que le motif se développe spatialement : toute perturbation telle qu'un changement de direction du vent ou collision de dune (cf. Fig. 1), constitue une graine amplifiée par l'instabilité linéaire au cours de la propagation.

tu.prop: As all modes propagate downwind, the instability is of convective type16 so that the pattern develops spatially: any disturbance such as a change of wind direction or a colliding dune (Fig. 1) constitutes a seed amplified by the linear instability in the course of propagation.

The histogram of these measurements, depicted on Fig. 3a, exhibits a clear peak around l < 20 m. The same measurements, performed on the windward side of the mega-barchan of Fig. 1d (40 m high), give a slightly larger wavelength of l < 28 m. This can be related to an increase of the wavelength--pattern coarsening--as the waves propagate, due to screenings and fusions.

tuv.seg: The maximum growth rate--the most unstable mode predicted by equation (2)--is reached for a wavelength l max < 12L < 20 m. l was measured systematically in a zone of 500 regular barchans (1­12 m high).

Le taux de croissance maximal (le mode le plus instable prédit par l'équation (2)) est atteint pour une longueur d'onde l max < 12L < 20 m. l a été mesuré systématiquement dans une zone de 500 barkhanes régulières (1-12 m de haut).

tu.prop: The maximum growth rate--the most unstable mode predicted by equation (2)--is reached for a wavelength l max < 12L < 20 m. l was measured systematically in a zone of 500 regular barchans (1­12 m high).
Situations where the fluid is much denser (water) or lighter (Mars atmosphere) than air allows a further examination of the scaling proposed in equation (1).

tuv.seg: The histogram of these measurements, depicted on Fig. 3a, exhibits a clear peak around l < 20 m. The same measurements, performed on the windward side of the mega-barchan of Fig. 1d (40 m high), give a slightly larger wavelength of l < 28 m. This can be related to an increase of the wavelength--pattern coarsening--as the waves propagate, due to screenings and fusions.

L'histogramme de ces mesures, représenté sur la figure 3a, présente un pic clair autour de l < 20 m. Les mêmes mesures, effectuées sur le versant au vent de la méga barkhane de la figure 1d (40 m de haut), donnent une longueur d'onde légèrement plus grande de l < 28 m. Cela peut être lié à une augmentation de la longueur d'onde (le grossissement du motif) à mesure que les ondes se propagent, en raison des phénomènes de masquage et de fusion.

tu.prop: The histogram of these measurements, depicted on Fig. 3a, exhibits a clear peak around l < 20 m. The same measurements, performed on the windward side of the mega-barchan of Fig. 1d (40 m high), give a slightly larger wavelength of l < 28 m. This can be related to an increase of the wavelength--pattern coarsening--as the waves propagate, due to screenings and fusions.
It gives the correct destabilization wavelength for submarine ripples in the turbulent case: l max reduces to a few centimetres, because water is 800 times denser than air.

tuv.seg: Situations where the fluid is much denser (water) or lighter (Mars atmosphere) than air allows a further examination of the scaling proposed in equation (1).
Les situations où le fluide est beaucoup plus dense (eau) ou plus léger (atmosphère de Mars) que l'air permettent un examen plus approfondi de la mise à l'échelle proposée dans l'équation (1).

tu.prop: Situations where the fluid is much denser (water) or lighter (Mars atmosphere) than air allows a further examination of the scaling proposed in equation (1).

Martian dunes17 present surface waves of wavelength around 600 m for an atmosphere 80 times lighter than Earth's. According to our analysis, the grains in saltation forming these dunes should have a diameter of 75 mm, which corresponds to the size of the dominant species composing the aeolian ripples photographed by the Mars rover Opportunity18.

tuv.seg: It gives the correct destabilization wavelength for submarine ripples in the turbulent case: l max reduces to a few centimetres, because water is 800 times denser than air.

Elle donne la longueur d'onde de déstabilisation correcte pour les ondulations sous-marines dans le cas turbulent : l max se réduit à quelques centimètres, car l'eau est 800 fois plus dense que l'air.

tu.prop: It gives the correct destabilization wavelength for submarine ripples in the turbulent case: l max reduces to a few centimetres, because water is 800 times denser than air.

As shown in Fig. 2a, the velocity of large barchans decreases with height H following the Bagnold relation1 c < aQ=H; where a < 2.7 represents the increase in flux between the ground and the brink and is measured independently (Supplementary Information).

tuv.seg: Martian dunes17 present surface waves of wavelength around 600 m for an atmosphere 80 times lighter than Earth's. According to our analysis, the grains in saltation forming these dunes should have a diameter of 75 mm, which corresponds to the size of the dominant species composing the aeolian ripples photographed by the Mars rover Opportunity18.

Les dunes martiennes17 présentent des ondes de surface avec une longueur d'onde d'environ 600 m pour une atmosphère 80 fois plus légère que celle de la Terre. Selon notre analyse, les grains en saltation formant ces dunes devraient avoir un diamètre de 75 mm, ce qui correspond à la taille des espèces dominantes composant les ondulations éoliennes photographiées par le rover martien Opportunity18.

tu.prop: Martian dunes17 present surface waves of wavelength around 600 m for an atmosphere 80 times lighter than Earth's. According to our analysis, the grains in saltation forming these dunes should have a diameter of 75 mm, which corresponds to the size of the dominant species composing the aeolian ripples photographed by the Mars rover Opportunity18.

Furthermore, we expect the velocity of barchans to match that of waves in the limit of vanishing amplitude, for which equation (2) predicts: c < cðlmax Þ < 5:3Q=L: This suggests that we should modify the Bagnold relation: c ¼ aQ=ðH þ H 0 Þ ð3Þ where H 0 ¼ aL=5:3 < 87 cm is a cut-off that corresponds to the minimal barchan height.

tuv.seg: As shown in Fig. 2a, the velocity of large barchans decreases with height H following the Bagnold relation1 c < aQ=H; where a < 2.7 represents the increase in flux between the ground and the brink and is measured independently (Supplementary Information).

Comme le montre la figure 2a, la vitesse des grosses barkhanes décroît avec la hauteur H selon la relation de Bagnold1 c < aQ=H ; où a < 2,7 représente l'augmentation du flux entre le sol et le crête, qui est mesurée séparément (cf. Informations supplémentaires).

tu.prop: As shown in Fig. 2a, the velocity of large barchans decreases with height H following the Bagnold relation1 c < aQ=H; where a < 2.7 represents the increase in flux between the ground and the brink and is measured independently (Supplementary Information).

Figure 2 | Wave-like behaviour of surface undulations. a, Comparison of the velocity c of waves and dunes as a function of their height H. The velocity of 140 dunes is measured using several aerial photographs and global positioning sytem (GPS) data, with displacements resolved to within 4 m. The velocity is averaged over two time intervals (300 to 350 months and 10 to 18 months) to sample both large dunes that do not propagate much over a year, and small ones that cannot be followed over long periods of time (see Fig. 1i).

tuv.seg: Furthermore, we expect the velocity of barchans to match that of waves in the limit of vanishing amplitude, for which equation (2) predicts: c < cðlmax Þ < 5:3Q=L: This suggests that we should modify the Bagnold relation: c ¼ aQ=ðH þ H 0 Þ ð3Þ where H 0 ¼ aL=5:3 < 87 cm is a cut-off that corresponds to the minimal barchan height.

De plus, nous nous attendons à ce que la vitesse des barkhanes corresponde à celle des ondes dans la limite d'une amplitude nulle, pour laquelle l'équation (2) prédit : c < cðlmax Þ < 5:3Q=L : Cela suggère que nous devrions modifier la relation de Bagnold : c ¼ aQ= ðH þ H 0 Þ ð3Þ, où H 0 ¼ aL=5:3 < 87 cm est un seuil qui correspond à la hauteur minimale d'une barkhane.

tu.prop: Furthermore, we expect the velocity of barchans to match that of waves in the limit of vanishing amplitude, for which equation (2) predicts: c < cðlmax Þ < 5:3Q=L: This suggests that we should modify the Bagnold relation: c ¼ aQ=ðH þ H 0 Þ ð3Þ where H 0 ¼ aL=5:3 < 87 cm is a cut-off that corresponds to the minimal barchan height.
The waves were marked with sticks and their displacements measured after a few hours.

tuv.seg: Figure 2 | Wave-like behaviour of surface undulations. a, Comparison of the velocity c of waves and dunes as a function of their height H. The velocity of 140 dunes is measured using several aerial photographs and global positioning sytem (GPS) data, with displacements resolved to within 4 m. The velocity is averaged over two time intervals (300 to 350 months and 10 to 18 months) to sample both large dunes that do not propagate much over a year, and small ones that cannot be followed over long periods of time (see Fig. 1i).

Figure 2 | Comportement ondulatoire des ondulations de surface. a, Comparaison de la vitesse c des ondes et des dunes en fonction de leur hauteur H. La vitesse de 140 dunes a été mesurée à l'aide de plusieurs photographies aériennes et de données du système de positionnement global (GPS), avec des déplacements résolus à près de 4 m. La vitesse est moyenné sur deux intervalles de temps (de 300 à 350 mois et de 10 à 18 mois) afin d'échantillonner à la fois les grandes dunes qui ne se déplacent pas beaucoup sur une année et les petites dunes qui ne peuvent pas être suivies sur de longues périodes (cf. Fig. 1i).

tu.prop: Figure 2 | Wave-like behaviour of surface undulations. a, Comparison of the velocity c of waves and dunes as a function of their height H. The velocity of 140 dunes is measured using several aerial photographs and global positioning sytem (GPS) data, with displacements resolved to within 4 m. The velocity is averaged over two time intervals (300 to 350 months and 10 to 18 months) to sample both large dunes that do not propagate much over a year, and small ones that cannot be followed over long periods of time (see Fig. 1i).

The corresponding velocities follow the same law as that of dunes, given a redefinition of Q: it is the saturated flux on solid ground averaged over the period of time considered in the case of dunes, and in the case of waves, the local saturated flux estimated from their altitude and from the propagation of neighbouring barchans (Supplementary Information).

tuv.seg: The waves were marked with sticks and their displacements measured after a few hours.
Les ondes ont été marquées avec des bâtons, et leurs déplacements ont été mesurés après quelques heures.

tu.prop: The waves were marked with sticks and their displacements measured after a few hours.
The purple solid line corresponds to the prediction of equation (3). b, Comparison of a wave profile dh with the corresponding modulations of sand flux dq.

tuv.seg: The corresponding velocities follow the same law as that of dunes, given a redefinition of Q: it is the saturated flux on solid ground averaged over the period of time considered in the case of dunes, and in the case of waves, the local saturated flux estimated from their altitude and from the propagation of neighbouring barchans (Supplementary Information).

Les vitesses correspondantes suivent la même loi que celle des dunes, à condition de redéfinir Q : dans le cas des dunes, il s'agit du flux saturé sur un sol solide, moyenné sur la période de temps considérée, et dans le cas des ondes, du flux saturé local estimé à partir de leur altitude et de la propagation des barkhanes voisines (cf. Informations complémentaires).

tu.prop: The corresponding velocities follow the same law as that of dunes, given a redefinition of Q: it is the saturated flux on solid ground averaged over the period of time considered in the case of dunes, and in the case of waves, the local saturated flux estimated from their altitude and from the propagation of neighbouring barchans (Supplementary Information).
Sticks are placed regularly along a cut of a barchan horn (yellow line on the photograph).

tuv.seg: The purple solid line corresponds to the prediction of equation (3). b, Comparison of a wave profile dh with the corresponding modulations of sand flux dq.
La ligne pleine violette correspond à la prédiction de l'équation (3). b, Comparaison d'un profil d'onde dh avec les modulations correspondantes du flux de sable dq.

tu.prop: The purple solid line corresponds to the prediction of equation (3). b, Comparison of a wave profile dh with the corresponding modulations of sand flux dq.

The slope and the distance between sticks allow for the reconstruction of the height profile dh.Ðdq is derived from the erosion rate >th, using mass conservation: dq ¼ 2 >t h dx (Supplementary Information).

tuv.seg: Sticks are placed regularly along a cut of a barchan horn (yellow line on the photograph).
Les bâtons sont placés régulièrement le long d'une coupe d'une corne de barkhane (ligne jaune sur la photographie).

tu.prop: Sticks are placed regularly along a cut of a barchan horn (yellow line on the photograph).

The oscillations of dq in phase with those of dh are characteristic of a plane propagating wave h(x 2 ct) for which dq ¼ Ð c >x h dx ¼ cdh: The local wave velocity c ¼ dq/dh decreases down-slope, like the local flux Q.

tuv.seg: The slope and the distance between sticks allow for the reconstruction of the height profile dh.Ðdq is derived from the erosion rate >th, using mass conservation: dq ¼ 2 >t h dx (Supplementary Information).

La pente et la distance entre les bâtons permettent la reconstruction du profil de hauteur dh. Ðdq est dérivé du taux d'érosion >th, en utilisant la conservation de masse : dq ¼ 2 >t h dx (cf. Informations supplémentaires).

tu.prop: The slope and the distance between sticks allow for the reconstruction of the height profile dh.Ðdq is derived from the erosion rate >th, using mass conservation: dq ¼ 2 >t h dx (Supplementary Information).

Figure 3 | Wavelength distribution and saturation length. a, Histograms of the wavelength l measured systematically in a zone of 20 km £ 8 km (solid line, 930 data points), as well as on the windward side of the mega-dune shown on Fig. 1d (dashed line, 850 data points), using 1-m bins.

tuv.seg: The oscillations of dq in phase with those of dh are characteristic of a plane propagating wave h(x 2 ct) for which dq ¼ Ð c >x h dx ¼ cdh: The local wave velocity c ¼ dq/dh decreases down-slope, like the local flux Q.

Les oscillations de dq en phase avec celles de dh sont caractéristiques d'une onde plane en propagation h(x 2 ct) pour laquelle dq ¼ Ð c >x h dx ¼ cdh: La vitesse de l'onde locale c ¼ dq/dh décroît en pente descendante, tout comme le flux local Q.

tu.prop: The oscillations of dq in phase with those of dh are characteristic of a plane propagating wave h(x 2 ct) for which dq ¼ Ð c >x h dx ¼ cdh: The local wave velocity c ¼ dq/dh decreases down-slope, like the local flux Q.
The peak of the distribution is reached for l ¼ 20 and 28 m respectively, which is consistent with the linear stability analysis.

tuv.seg: Figure 3 | Wavelength distribution and saturation length. a, Histograms of the wavelength l measured systematically in a zone of 20 km £ 8 km (solid line, 930 data points), as well as on the windward side of the mega-dune shown on Fig. 1d (dashed line, 850 data points), using 1-m bins.

Figure 3 | Distribution des longueur d'onde et longueur de saturation. a, Histogrammes des longueurs d'onde l mesurées systématiquement dans une zone de 20 km £ 8 km (ligne continue, 930 points de données), ainsi que sur le côté au vent de la méga-dune représentée sur la figure 1d (ligne en pointillée, 850 points de données), en utilisant des intervalles de 1 m.

tu.prop: Figure 3 | Wavelength distribution and saturation length. a, Histograms of the wavelength l measured systematically in a zone of 20 km £ 8 km (solid line, 930 data points), as well as on the windward side of the mega-dune shown on Fig. 1d (dashed line, 850 data points), using 1-m bins.
The histogram of the length of proto-barchans--dome dunes without slip face--is also superimposed (dotted line, 160 data points).

tuv.seg: The peak of the distribution is reached for l ¼ 20 and 28 m respectively, which is consistent with the linear stability analysis.
Le pic de la distribution est atteint pour l ¼ 20 et 28 m respectivement, ce qui est cohérent avec l'analyse de stabilité linéaire.

tu.prop: The peak of the distribution is reached for l ¼ 20 and 28 m respectively, which is consistent with the linear stability analysis.
It shows a peak at 14 m. b, Spatial transient of sand transport saturation.

tuv.seg: The histogram of the length of proto-barchans--dome dunes without slip face--is also superimposed (dotted line, 160 data points).
L'histogramme de la longueur des proto-barkhanes (dunes en dôme sans face de glissement) est également superposé (ligne pointillée, 160 points de données).

tu.prop: The histogram of the length of proto-barchans--dome dunes without slip face--is also superimposed (dotted line, 160 data points).
A 20-m-long sand sheet is prepared with a bulldozer on the solid ground and protected to insure a null flux at the upwind edge.

tuv.seg: It shows a peak at 14 m. b, Spatial transient of sand transport saturation.
Il montre un pic à 14 m. b, Transitoire spatial de la saturation du transport de sable.

tu.prop: It shows a peak at 14 m. b, Spatial transient of sand transport saturation.

As for the wave profiles (Fig. 2b), the flux is measured by integrating the erosion rate >th, averaged over 24 h. The data are well fitted by an exponential relaxation q ¼ Q½1 2 expð2x=LÞ, which corresponds to a first-order equation of saturation, L>x q ¼ Q 2 q:

tuv.seg: A 20-m-long sand sheet is prepared with a bulldozer on the solid ground and protected to insure a null flux at the upwind edge.
Une feuille de sable de 20 m de long est préparée avec un bulldozer sur le sol dur et protégée pour assurer un flux nul du côté au vent.

tu.prop: A 20-m-long sand sheet is prepared with a bulldozer on the solid ground and protected to insure a null flux at the upwind edge.
Field data (Fig. 2a) shows a quantitative agreement with this model equation for the slow motion of barchans on solid ground, but also for the fast motion of waves on the surface of dunes.

tuv.seg: As for the wave profiles (Fig. 2b), the flux is measured by integrating the erosion rate >th, averaged over 24 h. The data are well fitted by an exponential relaxation q ¼ Q½1 2 expð2x=LÞ, which corresponds to a first-order equation of saturation, L>x q ¼ Q 2 q:

En ce qui concerne les profils d'ondes (cf. Fig. 2b), le flux est mesuré en intégrant le taux d'érosion >th, moyenné sur 24 h. Les données sont bien ajustées par une relaxation exponentielle q ¼ Q½1 2 expð2x =LÞ, ce qui correspond à une équation de saturation du premier ordre, L>x q ¼ Q 2 q :

tu.prop: As for the wave profiles (Fig. 2b), the flux is measured by integrating the erosion rate >th, averaged over 24 h. The data are well fitted by an exponential relaxation q ¼ Q½1 2 expð2x=LÞ, which corresponds to a first-order equation of saturation, L>x q ¼ Q 2 q:
It demonstrates that large-amplitude waves behave as barchans.

tuv.seg: Field data (Fig. 2a) shows a quantitative agreement with this model equation for the slow motion of barchans on solid ground, but also for the fast motion of waves on the surface of dunes.

Les données de terrain (cf. Fig. 2a) montre un accord quantitatif avec ce modèle d'équation pour le mouvement lent des barkhanes sur un sol dur, mais aussi pour le mouvement rapide des ondes à la surface des dunes.

tu.prop: Field data (Fig. 2a) shows a quantitative agreement with this model equation for the slow motion of barchans on solid ground, but also for the fast motion of waves on the surface of dunes.
As for any convective instability, the amplitude of the waves depends on the amplitude of the disturbances applied to the dune, as well as on the length of the dune itself.

tuv.seg: It demonstrates that large-amplitude waves behave as barchans.
Cela démontre que les ondes de grande amplitude se comportent comme des barkhanes.

tu.prop: It demonstrates that large-amplitude waves behave as barchans.
This is well illustrated by the response of dunes to changes of wind direction.

tuv.seg: As for any convective instability, the amplitude of the waves depends on the amplitude of the disturbances applied to the dune, as well as on the length of the dune itself.
Comme pour toute instabilité convective, l'amplitude des ondes dépend de l'amplitude des perturbations appliquées à la dune, ainsi que de la longueur de la dune elle-même.

tu.prop: As for any convective instability, the amplitude of the waves depends on the amplitude of the disturbances applied to the dune, as well as on the length of the dune itself.
The medium-sized dune of Fig. 1c can remain almost undisturbed because it is sufficiently isolated and the wind sufficiently constant.

tuv.seg: This is well illustrated by the response of dunes to changes of wind direction.
La réaction des dunes aux changements de direction du vent en est une bonne illustration.

tu.prop: This is well illustrated by the response of dunes to changes of wind direction.
Whereas dunes of size l max are small enough to quickly readapt their shape (Fig. 1a), larger ones (,5­10l max) generate a certain number of waveforms in the lee flank (Fig. 1b, c).

tuv.seg: The medium-sized dune of Fig. 1c can remain almost undisturbed because it is sufficiently isolated and the wind sufficiently constant.
La dune de taille moyenne de la Figure 1c peut rester pratiquement inchangée parce qu'elle est suffisamment isolée et que le vent est relativement constant.

tu.prop: The medium-sized dune of Fig. 1c can remain almost undisturbed because it is sufficiently isolated and the wind sufficiently constant.
The mega-barchan of Fig. 1d is so large (30l max) that the daily wind variations (,158) render its surface permanently unstable.

tuv.seg: Whereas dunes of size l max are small enough to quickly readapt their shape (Fig. 1a), larger ones (,5­10l max) generate a certain number of waveforms in the lee flank (Fig. 1b, c).

Alors que les dunes de taille λmax sont suffisamment petites pour réadapter leur forme rapidement (cf. Fig. 1a), les plus grandes (5-10λ max) génèrent un certain nombre de formes d’ondes sur la face sous le vent (cf. Fig. 1b, c).

tu.prop: Whereas dunes of size l max are small enough to quickly readapt their shape (Fig. 1a), larger ones (,5­10l max) generate a certain number of waveforms in the lee flank (Fig. 1b, c).
Barchans and mega-barchans thus belong to the same class of dunes, the corrugations resulting from a size effect.

tuv.seg: The mega-barchan of Fig. 1d is so large (30l max) that the daily wind variations (,158) render its surface permanently unstable.
La méga barkhane de la Figure 1d est si grande (30λ max) que les variations quotidiennes du vent (~15°) rendent sa surface instable en permanence.

tu.prop: The mega-barchan of Fig. 1d is so large (30l max) that the daily wind variations (,158) render its surface permanently unstable.
A colliding dune constitutes another type of perturbation.

tuv.seg: Barchans and mega-barchans thus belong to the same class of dunes, the corrugations resulting from a size effect.
Les barkhanes et les méga-barkhanes appartiennent donc à la même catégorie de dunes, les ondulations résultant d'un effet de taille.

tu.prop: Barchans and mega-barchans thus belong to the same class of dunes, the corrugations resulting from a size effect.
Figure 1g shows a typical example of coaxial collision between two large dunes (3.5l max and 6l max), one catching the other up from behind.

tuv.seg: A colliding dune constitutes another type of perturbation.
Une dune en collision constitue un autre type de perturbation.

tu.prop: A colliding dune constitutes another type of perturbation.

Displaying behaviour that is far from soliton-like (solitons are propagative solitary waves which do not interact with each other), the impacting dune induces large-amplitude waves on the flanks of the target dune, which amplify in the course of their propagation.

tuv.seg: Figure 1g shows a typical example of coaxial collision between two large dunes (3.5l max and 6l max), one catching the other up from behind.
La Figure 1g montre un exemple typique de collision coaxiale entre deux grandes dunes (3.5 λmax et 6 λmax), l’une rattrapant l'autre par derrière.

tu.prop: Figure 1g shows a typical example of coaxial collision between two large dunes (3.5l max and 6l max), one catching the other up from behind.
Figure 1i shows that these waves not only decorate the dunes, but play a crucial part in the long-term evolution of a barchan field.

tuv.seg: Displaying behaviour that is far from soliton-like (solitons are propagative solitary waves which do not interact with each other), the impacting dune induces large-amplitude waves on the flanks of the target dune, which amplify in the course of their propagation.

La dune impactante adopte un comportement éloigné de celui du soliton (les solitons sont des ondes solitaires propagatives qui n’interagissent pas entre elles), elle induit des ondes de grande amplitude sur les flancs de la dune cible, qui s’amplifient au cours de leur propagation.

tu.prop: Displaying behaviour that is far from soliton-like (solitons are propagative solitary waves which do not interact with each other), the impacting dune induces large-amplitude waves on the flanks of the target dune, which amplify in the course of their propagation.
When a wave reaches solid ground at the end of the horn, an elementary dune of typical size slightly smaller than l max (Fig. 3a) is detached and emitted.

tuv.seg: Figure 1i shows that these waves not only decorate the dunes, but play a crucial part in the long-term evolution of a barchan field.
La figure 1i montre que ces ondes ne font pas que décorer les dunes mais elles jouent un rôle crucial dans l’évolution du champ de barkhanes sur le long terme.

tu.prop: Figure 1i shows that these waves not only decorate the dunes, but play a crucial part in the long-term evolution of a barchan field.
Globally, this produces numerous newborn dunes in the wake of the collision and thus a large sand loss from the procreative dunes.

tuv.seg: When a wave reaches solid ground at the end of the horn, an elementary dune of typical size slightly smaller than l max (Fig. 3a) is detached and emitted.
Lorsqu'une onde atteint le sol solide à l’extrémité de la corne, une dune élémentaire de taille généralement légèrement inférieure à λmax (cf. Fig. 3a) se détache et est émise.

tu.prop: When a wave reaches solid ground at the end of the horn, an elementary dune of typical size slightly smaller than l max (Fig. 3a) is detached and emitted.

There are only two circumstances under which this collision mechanism apparently looks like a non-interactive process (1) the case where the two interacting dunes are themselves of lengths comparable to the elementary size l max and (2) the case where they only brush against each other (Fig. 1f).

tuv.seg: Globally, this produces numerous newborn dunes in the wake of the collision and thus a large sand loss from the procreative dunes.
En règle générale, ce phénomène produit de multiples dunes nouvelles dans le sillage de la collision, entraînant ainsi une importante perte de sable des dunes procréatrices.

tu.prop: Globally, this produces numerous newborn dunes in the wake of the collision and thus a large sand loss from the procreative dunes.
We have demonstrated8 that dunes behaving as stable kinematic waves would not lead to the size selection observed in barchan fields.

tuv.seg: There are only two circumstances under which this collision mechanism apparently looks like a non-interactive process (1) the case where the two interacting dunes are themselves of lengths comparable to the elementary size l max and (2) the case where they only brush against each other (Fig. 1f).

Il n'existe que deux circonstances dans lesquelles ce mécanisme de collision semble ne pas être un processus interactif, (1) lorsque les deux dunes en interaction ont elles-mêmes des longueurs comparables à la taille élémentaire λmax, et (2) lorqu'elles ne font que se frôler (cf. Fig. 1f).

tu.prop: There are only two circumstances under which this collision mechanism apparently looks like a non-interactive process (1) the case where the two interacting dunes are themselves of lengths comparable to the elementary size l max and (2) the case where they only brush against each other (Fig. 1f).
On the contrary, the whole dune field would merge into a single giant dune.

tuv.seg: We have demonstrated8 that dunes behaving as stable kinematic waves would not lead to the size selection observed in barchan fields.
Nous avons démontré8 que des dunes se comportant comme des ondes cinématiques stables ne conduisaient pas à la sélection de taille observée dans les champs de barkhanes.

tu.prop: We have demonstrated8 that dunes behaving as stable kinematic waves would not lead to the size selection observed in barchan fields.

To resolve this problem, there should exist a sand leak increasing with the dune size: our field study shows that the induction of waves on the surface of dunes, breaking the horns into elementary barchans, provides this missing mechanism.

tuv.seg: On the contrary, the whole dune field would merge into a single giant dune.
Au contraire, tout les champs de dunes fusionneraient en une unique dune géante.

tu.prop: On the contrary, the whole dune field would merge into a single giant dune.

tuv.seg: To resolve this problem, there should exist a sand leak increasing with the dune size: our field study shows that the induction of waves on the surface of dunes, breaking the horns into elementary barchans, provides this missing mechanism.

Pour résoudre ce problème, il devrait exister une fuite de sable augmentant avec la taille de la dune : notre étude sur le terrain montre que l'induction d'ondes sur la surface des dunes brisant les cornes en barkhanes élémentaires, fournit ce mécanisme qui manquait.
